name: Hervidero Waterfall and El Mesto Canal Trail
name_original: Cascada del Hervidero por el Canal del Mesto – circular desde San Agustín de Guadalix
description: Hike the moderate 9.0-mile Hervidero Waterfall and El Mesto Canal Trail, featuring a scenic waterfall and historic canal near San Agustín de
region_intro: Hiking around San Agustín Del Guadalix offers diverse landscapes within a protected natural environment. More than half of the municipality's territory is safeguarded natural land, featuring the Guadalix River and its canyon, Mediterranean scrubland, and holm oaks. The region's terrain includes riverside forests, open fields, and more rugged areas, providing varied experiences for hikers. It also serves as a gateway to larger natural parks like Sierra de Guadarrama. Best hiking trails around San Agustín Del Guadalix The most popular hiking route is Hervidero Waterfall and El Mesto Canal Trail, an 8.96 miles (14.4 km) moderate trail that takes 4 hours 3 minutes to complete. This route leads to the double Hervidero Waterfall on the Guadalix River. Another top favourite among local hikers is Route of the Aqueducts, a moderate 9.41 miles (15.1 km) path. This circular route passes by the Higueras Dam, the river canyon, and 19th-century aqueducts. Local hikers also love the El Coso Waterfall – Becerra Waterfall loop from San Agustín del Guadalix, a 4.49 miles (7.2 km) trail leading through riverside areas to two waterfalls, often completed in about 1 hour 56 minutes. Hiking around San Agustín Del Guadalix is defined by riverside forests, Mediterranean scrubland, and historical aqueducts. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy family walks to more strenuous treks. The routes in San Agustín Del Guadalix are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 280 reviews. More than 1400 hikers have used komoot to explore San Agustín Del Guadalix's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in San Agustín Del Guadalix?
A: San Agustín Del Guadalix offers a wide selection of routes, with over 40 hiking trails to explore. These range from easy strolls to more challenging treks, catering to various fitness levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around San Agustín Del Guadalix?
A: The terrain in San Agustín Del Guadalix is diverse, featuring riverside forests along the Guadalix River, Mediterranean scrubland with holm and cork oaks, and open fields offering expansive views. You'll also find routes through rocky canyons and past historical aqueducts, providing a constantly changing landscape.

Q: Are there any waterfalls I can visit on a hike in San Agustín Del Guadalix?
A: Yes, the area is known for its beautiful waterfalls. The most prominent is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6344971" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hervidero Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; on the Guadalix River. You can reach it via routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e956888425/hervidero-waterfall-and-el-mesto-canal-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hervidero Waterfall and El Mesto Canal Trail&lt;/a&gt;. Another popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14894471" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Coso Waterfall – Becerra Waterfall loop from San Agustín del Guadalix&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you past both the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6317937" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Coso Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and the Becerra Waterfall.

Q: Can I see historical landmarks like aqueducts on the trails?
A: Absolutely. San Agustín Del Guadalix features routes that highlight its rich hydraulic heritage. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e957836132/route-of-the-aqueducts" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Aqueducts&lt;/a&gt; is a notable circular hike that passes by the Higueras Dam and several 19th-century aqueducts, including those of La Retuerta, Valcaliente, and La Sima.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in San Agustín Del Guadalix?
A: Yes, many trails are suitable for families. The path to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6344971" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hervidero Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, is an easy, relatively flat round trip, making it a great choice for all ages. The region offers numerous easy routes, with 19 trails classified as easy, perfect for a leisurely family outing.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, San Agustín Del Guadalix has several excellent circular routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e957836132/route-of-the-aqueducts" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Aqueducts&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate circular hike. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14894471" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Coso Waterfall – Becerra Waterfall loop from San Agustín del Guadalix&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easier loop through riverside areas.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in San Agustín Del Guadalix?
A: The trails in San Agustín Del Guadalix are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.5 stars from over 280 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse landscapes, the beauty of the waterfalls, and the well-maintained paths that blend natural beauty with historical elements.

Q: Are there any viewpoints with panoramic views in San Agustín Del Guadalix?
A: Yes, you can find several spots offering impressive vistas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/649628" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Guadalix Canyon from Matahonda Rocks&lt;/a&gt; provides a great perspective of the river's course. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4905960" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pedrezuela Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; offers expansive views, especially towards the Sierra de Guadarrama.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails?
A: While specific regulations can vary, many natural areas in Spain, including those around San Agustín Del Guadalix, are generally dog-friendly, especially if dogs are kept on a leash. It's always recommended to check local signage or specific tour descriptions for any restrictions, particularly in protected areas or near wildlife.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in San Agustín Del Guadalix?
A: San Agustín Del Guadalix offers enjoyable hiking experiences throughout the year. Spring is particularly striking when water levels are high at the waterfalls and the vegetation is lush. Autumn provides beautiful colors in the riverside forests. Even in cooler months, the varied terrain and protected natural environment make for pleasant walks, especially on sunny days.

Q: Are there any lakes or reservoirs to explore near San Agustín Del Guadalix?
A: Yes, the region features several serene water bodies. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4905960" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pedrezuela Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; (also known as El Vellón) is a large, tranquil spot perfect for relaxed strolls with expansive views. You might also encounter smaller lagoons like Laguna de los Patos along the Guadalix River, offering peaceful spots for nature observation.

Q: How long do the hiking trails typically take to complete?
A: Hiking durations vary significantly depending on the route's length and difficulty. For example, an easy trail like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14894471" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Coso Waterfall – Becerra Waterfall loop from San Agustín del Guadalix&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in under 2 hours. Moderate trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e956888425/hervidero-waterfall-and-el-mesto-canal-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hervidero Waterfall and El Mesto Canal Trail&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e957836132/route-of-the-aqueducts" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Aqueducts&lt;/a&gt;, typically take around 4 to 4.5 hours.

Be careful when going down the narrow stairs that have no railings. The ascent is a bit poorly indicated, you have to look a little.

The weir is part of what used to be a reservoir to divert water from the Guadalix River to Madrid back in the early 20th century. Today only ruins remain and it is one of the icons of the well-known Hervidero route.

faqs.question: Where can I park and start the Hervidero Waterfall and El Mesto Canal Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is often undertaken as a circular route starting from San Agustín de Guadalix. You'll typically find parking options within or near the town, which serves as the main access point for this popular hike.

faqs.question: Is the Hervidero Waterfall and El Mesto Canal Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is dog-friendly! You can bring your dog along, but please ensure they are kept on a leash throughout the hike.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed year-round, but spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. In warmer months, the natural pool at the waterfall provides a great opportunity to cool off. Be aware that mornings can be humid and cool due to the trail's west-facing orientation along the cornice.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to access the Hervidero Waterfall and El Mesto Canal Trail. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the trail?
faqs.answer: The full circular route, which is approximately 14.4 kilometers (8.9 miles) long, generally takes around 4 hours and 4 minutes to complete at a moderate hiking pace.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain and scenery can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: You'll experience a diverse mix of natural paths, unpaved sections, and some paved areas. The route follows the Guadalix river through lush vegetation, including pines, oaks, poplars, and rockroses. A significant part of the trail runs along the El Mesto Weir, bordering a canyon with an exposed cornice.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The primary highlight is the beautiful Hervidero Waterfall, known for its natural pool. You'll also encounter the historical El Mesto Weir and aqueduct, offering scenic views and a glimpse into local engineering.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: While generally considered a moderate hike requiring good fitness, many paths are easily accessible. The route is mostly comfortable with little unevenness, making it accessible for families. However, some sections along the El Mesto Canal can be rocky and narrow, and there are narrow stairs without railings, so sure-footedness is advised.

faqs.question: Are there any challenging or unique sections on the trail I should be aware of?
faqs.answer: Yes, parts of the El Mesto Canal trail run along an exposed cornice bordering a canyon. While generally wide enough, some sections lack railings. There are also narrow stairs without railings and some rocky, narrow sections, especially along the Canal del Mesto, which might require extra care, particularly in wet conditions.

faqs.question: Can I cycle or trail run on this route?
faqs.answer: Yes, this is a multi-use trail popular for hiking, trail running, mountain biking, and e-biking. However, be aware that some rocky and narrow sections along the Canal del Mesto might be challenging for cyclists, potentially requiring dismounting, especially when wet.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Canal de El Mesto, Camino del Carril de las Mentiras, Camino Canal del Vellón, and Camino Canal Bajo CYII.
